title: ilex wattii subsp. wattii
intro: Ilex wattii subsp. wattii, also known as Taiwan holly or Chinese holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, and is often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Ilex wattii subsp. wattii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a remedy.
